title: Direct Materials Variances
event: Reyes Co. used 5,200 pounds of material at an actual price of $3.10 per pound. Standards allow 5,000 pounds at $3.00 for the output achieved. Compute the price and quantity variances.

computationPaths.narration: Split the total miss into two accountable pieces: purchasing owns price, production owns quantity.

memorize.body: Price variance = AQ × (AP − SP). Quantity variance = SP × (AQ − SQ).
Actual quantity anchors the PRICE variance; standard price anchors the QUANTITY variance — the terms cross.
Favorable/unfavorable by direction, not by math sign: paid more or used more = Unfavorable.

questions.prompt: AQ 5,200 lbs, AP $3.10, SP $3.00, SQ 5,000 lbs. Materials QUANTITY variance = ?

distractors.feedback: You priced the quantity gap at ACTUAL price (3.10 × 200). The quantity variance uses STANDARD price: 3.00 × 200 = 600.
That's the PRICE variance (5,200 × $0.10). Quantity measures the 200 extra pounds at standard: 600.
